performed (or taught) safely and effectively. Where these services are reasonable and 
necessary to treat the illness or injury, they may be covered. For these services to be 
reasonable and necessary, the medication being administered must be accepted as safe 
and effective treatment of the patient's illness or injury, and there must be a medical 
reason that the medication cannot be taken orally. Moreover, the frequency and duration
